title: Utricularia vitellina
taxonomy: Utricularia vitellina is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of bladderwort, specifically belonging to the family Lentibulariaceae. This family is further grouped under the order Lamiales in the angiosperm division of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is a part of the clade Asterids in the larger group called the eudicots. Utricularia vitellina is a carnivorous plant native to certain regions. Its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary adaptations.

intro: Utricularia vitellina, also known as the Yellow Bladderwort, is a carnivorous plant species belonging to the bladderwort family (Lentibulariaceae). Traditionally, Utricularia vitellina has been used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal preparations, Utricularia vitellina is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other plants, to create adaptogenic blends and digestive remedies.
